title: CCPA/CPRA — Automated Decision Profiling — Feature Drift Alerts on Consumer-Profile Models

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'unspecified' → '§1798.185'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Model observability HEC (`whylogs`, Evidently, or custom)
spl:
index=ml sourcetype="profile:feature_snapshot" model_name="marketing_propensity" earliest=-120d
| eval sd=strptime(snapshot_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| sort feature_name, sd
| streamstats window=2 global=f first(mean_val) as prev_mean by feature_name
| eval delta_pct=if(isnotnull(prev_mean) AND prev_mean!=0, abs(mean_val-prev_mean)/abs(prev_mean)*100, null())
| where delta_pct>25
| table feature_name, sd, prev_mean, mean_val, delta_pct
value: Profiling systems should not silently change behavior. Tracking population-level distribution shifts in model inputs (e.g., spend deciles) supports governance that consumers receive consistent, explainable outcomes relative to disclosures.
implementation: (1) Calibrate `delta_pct` with model risk team; (2) exclude planned campaign seasonality via lookup; (3) trigger model card update workflow; (4) document consumer-facing impact assessment; (5) pair with fairness testing outside Splunk.
visualization: Line chart (mean_val over time), Table (drift events), Single value (features drifted).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
